steps.title: Understand what no solution means for linear systems
steps.content: A system of linear equations has no solution when the two equations represent parallel lines — they have identical slopes but different y-intercepts. Geometrically, parallel lines never intersect.

steps.title: Rewrite the first equation to identify its form
steps.content:
Given $2x + 6y = 10$, divide both sides by 2:
$$x + 3y = 5$$
Any equation parallel to this will have the form $x + 3y = k$ where $k \neq 5$. If $k = 5$, the equations are identical (infinite solutions); if $k \neq 5$, they are parallel (no solution).

steps.title: Check each choice for the parallel form
steps.content:
Choice A: $x + 3y = 5$ — This equals our simplified equation, so it represents the same line (infinite solutions, not no solution).

Choice B: $x + 3y = -20$ — Same left side as the simplified form, but the right side is $-20 \neq 5$. These lines are parallel!

Choice C: $6x - 2y = 0$ — The coefficients of $x$ and $y$ are not proportional to those in the original equation, so this has a different slope.

Choice D: $6x + 2y = 10$ — The coefficients are not proportional to the original, so this also has a different slope.

Only choice B gives us two parallel lines with no solution. That matches choice B — B is correct.
